title: Al Williamson
content:
{{Top|rwp}}
{{Person
|image=[[File:William4.png]]
|name=Al Williamson
|birth=[[March 21]], [[1930s#1931|1931]]
|death=[[June 12]], [[2010]]<ref>{{WebCite|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2010/06/21/arts/design/21williamson.html?_r=0|text=Al Williamson, Illustrator of Comic Books, Dies at 79|date=2010-06-21|author=[https://www.nytimes.com/by/dennis-hevesi Hevesi, Dennis]|work=[[Wikipedia:The New York Times|The New York Times]]|archivedate=20180723003603}}</ref>
|listedpronouns=<!--Only include pronouns if they have been explicitly specified by the individual to which this article pertains.-->
|nationality=
|occupation=Illustrator
|companies=[[Marvel Comics]]
|website=
}}
'''Al Williamson''' was a comic-book illustrator. He worked on the ''[[Star Wars]]'' daily [[Star Wars (comic strip)|comic strips]], as well as the comic-book adaptations for [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']] published by [[Marvel Comics]].

In [[1950s|1952]] Williamson began working at [[Wikipedia:EC Comics|EC Comics]] on their [[Wikipedia:Weird Science (comic)|''Weird Science'']] and [[Wikipedia:Weird Fantasy|''Weird Fantasy'']] titles. In the 1960s he worked on the moderately successful line of comic magazines published by [[Wikipedia:Warren Publishing|Warren Publishing]], and later went on to draw the [[Wikipedia:Flash Gordon|''Flash Gordon'']] comic book for [[Wikipedia:King Comics|King Comics]]. In the late 1960s Williamson teamed up with writer [[Archie Goodwin]], and together they produced the [[Wikipedia:Secret Agent X-9|''Secret Agent X-9'']] syndicated comic strip, and would later work together again on the comic adaptation of ''The Empire Strikes Back'' for Marvel Comics. After [[Russ Manning]] died in the early 1980s, Goodwin and Williamson took over the ''Star Wars'' syndicated comic strip, and the same team produced the comic adaptation of ''Return of the Jedi'' for Marvel. In the 1990s Williamson became a regular inker on various Marvel titles including working for several years on the [[Wikipedia:Daredevil (Marvel Comics)|''Daredevil'']] title. He also did the inking for the [[Dark Horse Comics]] adaptation of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''The Phantom Menace'']].
